🐹 Go for Vibe Coders Contents Cheatsheet
🐹

Golang for Vibe Coders

A first-principles tour of Go — the small, boring, blazingly practical language — and why its one-way-to-do-things plainness makes it the best partner for an AI coding agent.

narrated in the voice of Richard Feynman · first-principles, intuition-first

▶ Start reading ⚡ Cheatsheet

Here's the funny thing about "vibe coding" — you describe what you want in plain English, an agent writes the code, and you mostly judge the result. So the question stops being "which language is most powerful?" and becomes "which language is easiest to get right — for a machine to write, for a human to skim, and for both to trust?" That language is Go. It has one way to format code, one way to handle an error, one tool that does everything, and a compiler fast enough to be part of the conversation. Read these in order; each chapter is a brick the next one stands on.

Table of Contents

  1. Why Go, and Why Now Where Go genuinely wins — and why its plainness is a superpower when an agent holds the pen. the why
  2. The Five-Minute Mental Model The whole shape of a Go program: packages, functions, types — and go run. basics
  3. Goroutines & Channels Doing a thousand things at once, made almost boring. Go's headline feature. concurrency
  4. Errors Are Just Values No exceptions, no hidden control flow. Failure is a value you handle in the open. idioms
  5. Structs, Methods & Interfaces Go's quiet take on types: data, behavior, and duck-typing that the compiler checks. types
  6. A Real Web API — Standard Library Only Build a JSON HTTP service with zero dependencies using the modern net/http router. build
  7. Vibe Coding Go with Agents The actual workflow: specs, the agent loop, and the tooling that keeps the AI honest. workflow
  8. Shipping: Modules, Tooling & One Binary From go mod init to a single static binary you can scp to a box and run. ship
🐹
About this book

Researched from the official Go documentation, blog, and release notes, and verified against Go 1.26 (released February 2026) — the release that made the Green Tea garbage collector the default. The "vibe coding" framing follows Andrej Karpathy's original February 2025 coinage. Everything here is self-contained and reads offline. Press ⌘R or reopen the book to refresh after edits.
